sourceMovie.title: Frida
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Frida works as a drama movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The drama and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: CODA
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Frida clicked for you, CODA (2021) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and romance, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Ruby Sparks
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Frida clicked for you, Ruby Sparks (2012) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and romance, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Frida?
faq.answer: Try CODA, Ruby Sparks, Across the Universe—each shares DNA with Frida while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Frida?
faq.answer: Frida (2002) is best described as Drama, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Frida worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
